Issue No.20 • March 2013
A Big 01' Fatty Swipe File
Of En1ail Openings
Dearest Email Player,
So I sometimes think more people struggle with writing a good opening sentence/
paragraph with their emails than they do any other part (including the subject line).
Why this is, I don't know exactly.
I've always found it pretty easy.
But, it's a question that often rolls in and so I'm going to hand you (on a golden
platter) a list of email opens that can be universally used and/ or adapted for just about
any product, market or list. These are not necessarily the "sexiest" openings. In fact, I
rarely go for super clever. I take a page from the Gene Schwartz school of copywriting --
where my ONLY goal is to get them to read the next sentence. That MIGHT mean
saying something clever or funny or whatever. But a lot of these are just meant to plant an
incomplete thought or idea in their head so they feel the urge to keep reading. Again,
these are universally applicable openings. And if you are ever stuck for ideas, just reach
into this issue and pull one out. You could even use it for the rest of your email writing
life. Just go through the list each day and pick them out in order. You can also play with
them however you want. Do what you need to do based on what you know about your

A subscriber pops the question:
Because I'm running low on hate mail ... 	[Make this a controversial email,
and with this opening you "prep" them for it]

Whoa! 	[Great for the email you send the day after a really

controversial email]
Oh how people weep & gnash their teeth over unsubscribes. 	[Follow with
email telling them why they should not be worried about something they
are all worried about]
I want to switch things up a bit today.
Hey, 	so no time for messing around today.
So I 	had a brain fart yesterday.

But these ALONE should be more than enough to make writing that all-important
first sentence as easy as 1-2-3. Remember, there's nothing really "clever" about any of

Just do this stream of conscious. Mix it up and always be unpredictable.

Why Split Testing Emails
Is Still (Mosdy)
A Fat Waste Of Time
Last year I took a lot of "flack" from my list (actually, just the goo-roo fan boys on
my list) for saying you cannot scientifically test emails. The reason why I said it is because
a real scientific test is done multiple times, in a controlled environment, where there are
NO changing variables. These conditions simply don't exist in email, no matter how
much someone wants it to be otherwise.
Hey, I didn't invent this or pull it out of my bootay.
I never was a big email tester (since I am a big believer in money being attracted to
SPEED, not dilly-dallying around with auto-responder software designed by
programmers and not direct marketers).
So I was pleasantly surprised when I was taught this whole "testing emails is a
waste of time" by a computer scientist friend, and have had it backed up by other true
scientists who also laugh whenever they hear someone brag about all their supposed
-5-

www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 19 --

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
"scientific email tests." Fact is, if you ARE split testing emails, and if you ARE blindly
believing the so-called "in tel" those tests are giving you ... then you could be shooting
yourself in the foot. I can tell you this from personal experience (and having done it
dozens of times) that you can re-use a "winner" email later and watch it bomb. But, even
more important, you can re-use a "loser" email and watch it own. There are just too
many changing variables for an email test to be totally reliable.
Yes, you can (and should) re-use emails that got you lots of sales.
But don't assume they'll kill it again.
And, don't assume a loser email won't kill it next time.
What's that? You want MORE proof I speaketh the troof here?
Okay, shortly after sending out an email talking about this subject I got this email
from "Email Players" subscriber Sanjay Pande who has designed and conducted many of
these so-called "tests", and even he says the idea of testing emails is, well, BS:
Thanks for this. 	It's really funny.
As you know I'm the geek who has designed and built many of these
so called tests, 	I 	can tell you your scientist friend is 100%
correct.
There are way too many variables in e-mails.
1. E-mail volume is relatively small. 	The larger the volume the
margin for error goes up anyway.
2. 	Split testing subject lines is useless because you may have
different delivery or open rates.
3. 	It's hard 	(if not impossible) 	to tie an e-mail to sales unless
the offer is in the e-mail. 	Even then you don't know what
"really" caused the sale. 	Sometimes it's the sequence. 	Sometimes
it's people's mood. 	There are a number of causal effects before
the sale. 	The prior e-mail could have been more of an influencer
of the sale.
4. A price change 	(even an increase) can put your sales up or
down. 	The e-mail in this case was not the cause of the sale and
-6-

all the split tests in the world wouldn't work 	(Ted Nicholas book
flopped at 20 bucks and was a best-seller at 70 bucks to the same
lists).
s. 	Even the average marketer knows that 80% of sales are made
after the 5th contact. 	So, what's the real point of your split
test on an e-mail with an offer? This is a problem that even
plagues direct mail marketers.
6. 	Most tests are done in smaller numbers with the premise that
rolling up will replicate the results. 	This is flawed from a
scientific perspective again. 	The sample size changed
dramatically which will affect the results and you'll never know
why you had such a big hit or a flop - 	even though it's helpful
to have indicators to go on.
All these folks who spout their expertise on testing should
really talk to a few scientific people (and perhaps geeks) 	on how
tests are done and how they still mean squat.
People who think they're marketers are the worst offenders
followed by the folks who call themselves "real" business people
- 	very few who even understand how these things even work.
Tests will only give you "indicators" and as you said, 	you really
do not know when the same e-mail will work or bomb when re-used
(with your evidence).
Thanks for covering this topic. 	People really should wake up and
get it.
Warm Regards,
Sanjay Pande
'nuff said!
The moral of the story here is to not get caught up in the latest goo-roo blathering
on about all their tests or whatever. The real power in email is in being consistent and
persistent. Getting out there every day, plugging your product, and positioning yourself as
a leader (and not just a mere expert). All of which will happen automatically when you
apply what you learn in these newsletters each month.

''I Am Iron Man''
My all-time favorite business books is Ken McCarthy's "System Club Letters" (the
book enclosed with this newsletter). Believe it or not, that book has been in my bathroom
since january of 2008 and I've been reading a page or two every time I go in there to,
uhm, "take care of bid'niz". Every time I read it I get some new insight, idea or strategy I
can use to profit with.
One of the best lessons inside is the chapter he wrote about the Ironman
competition. As Ken puts it, every year people voluntarily pay money to endure treatment
and pain that would make Attila the Hun wince at it. His point being, there are people
out there who are not content with just being average. Who want to separate themselves
from the bleeting herd of man-zombies in the world and do crazy stuff most people can't
even dream of doing.
And, he said there are people on YOUR list like this.
And, he also said if you simply put an offer in front of them that's so ballzy and
outrageous and (yes) expensive, that it even takes YOUR breath away, you may be
shocked by how much money you can make.
And the rest of your list who want's nothing to do with it?
They'll be content to sit back and buy the t-shirt and popcorn.
Everyone wins.
Anyway, when you get into this mindset you can use email to test out your
"Ironman" ideas pretty easily.
For example: A couple years ago I thought I might be interested in doing coaching.
So I sent out the following email (that took maybe 20 minutes to write) and no sales letter
or anything like that. I just wanted to test the waters to see what kind of interest there
was. (And by the way, I think you will find this pretty easy to model for anything you want
to test -- it's based on 100% transparency and worked pretty well):
-8-

Subject: To Coach, 	or Not To coach, 	That Is The Question .•.
I'm putting some "feelers" out for something.
For years, 	people have asked if I do personal coaching (mostly
for email marketing and copywriting). 	I 	have actively resisted
this for a multitude of reasons.
But lately, 	have been toying with it more.
It would probably entail things like:
1. Giving unlimited email access to me with your questions
about your ads, 	emails, marketing ideas, 	strategies, 	etc
2. Access to me by phone once per week for 30 minutes. 	Plus, 	you
would have the rights to any of these coaching calls to use for
your own use, 	to sell them (private label them, 	whatever). 	So if
you sell business-to-business, 	you would have ready-made products
over time
3. Access to all my products 	(if you committed to a few months in
advance)
4. 	Special group trainings 	(all coaching clients on the same
line, 	learning, 	asking questions, 	etc)
5. Guidance on your emails and ads as you do them-- you could
literally send me your emails each day (I would show you how to
do them) 	before sending them out and I'd go through them and make
sure you are on the right track
Obviously, 	this would be for real business owners, 	only.
And it would NOT be for newbies 	(even if you can afford it).
This would be for people who have a 	list, 	have a product, 	are
making sales and want to make MORE of the green stuff.
In other words, 	a sales multiplier.
Not a magic push button business or done-for-you service.
Plus, 	it would not be cheap, 	either.
I'm thinking at least a few thousand dollars per month.

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
How One Of The World's
Highest Paid Copywriters
Persuades People To Believe
Even Super Outrageous Clai:ms
One of the top direct mail copywriters on planet Earth is a guy named Parris
Lampropoulos. I don't know the dude personally, but I have seen his ads and have read
some of his teachings and listened in on a "round table" talk he did once with some other
copywriters/direct marketers (discussing the late copywriter Gene Schwartz). The guy has
some skillz, no doubt. But probably the most powerful thing I learned from him was the
way he structures his ads for maximum proof and credibility. In the world of direct mail
this is mucho important. If you think about it, in direct mail you are mailing your pitch to
ice cold prospects who don't have a clue who you are (a complete stranger), to ask them to
buy something from you they didn't even know existed 5 minutes ago. A tall order indeed.
In email we can get away with a little slop simply because we're in front of our prospects
so much and they grow to trust us and (hopefully) like us. And that's why, if you use
Parris's proof structure in your emails (especially when doing a sale or want to make a
more blatant pitch) it can do some seriously good things for your bank account. So
anyway, first I'll show you his proof structure. Then, I'll show you an example of an email
that uses it (and don't worry, this is almost laughably easy-- just the way I like it!). Okay,
so here goes:
Story·· You start with a story about someone who had the problem your
product or services solves. Pretty self explanatory. It can be a story about
you or someone else, doesn't really matter. What's important to realize is
people are sort of "hard wired" to be persuaded by stories neurologically.
Plus, a story is naturally entertaining and interesting and emotional. And so
after telling the story you go to the ...
Mechanis1n •• This is the thing that makes your product or service or
whatever work. What makes it unique. What makes people have confidence
they aren't just getting more of the same old, same old. So for example, if
you sell a weight loss book, you could talk about about how taking showers
helps with burning ofT weight. The mechanism is the shower (of course, you
-11-
www.BenSettlc.com 	• 	Copyright 2013 Ben Settle, All Rights Reserved 	• 	Published By Settle, LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 19 --

THE 	EMAIL PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
want to be as VAGUE as possible about how it works in this case, you don't
want to give the tip away -- but just enough to get them wanting to buy).
Mter that, you put the final nail in the coffin with the...
Studies/hard data- These are the hard FACTS that support your
argument. They are statistics, test results, numbers, etc. In our example
above you talk about how if you take a shower in a specific way mentioned
in the book, research shows your body reacts by burning calories, etc and
you show them the science behind it (while still talking in "plain English" of
course).
And there you have it -- the exact proof structure used by one of the world's top
direct mail copywriters. I know... I know! Not exactly the most mind-blowing formula, is
it? Hey, don't let that simplicity fool you! It's extremely powerful. And, yes, it can (and
should when practical) be used in emails, like this ...
Subject Line: 	"Kick start" 	fat 	loss by standing in cold water
Let me tell you a quick story about fat loss:
Not long ago one of my friends came to me saying she hates
exercise and has trouble doing it because of various health
problems. 	And so she wanted a quick solution to at least get her
fat 	loss "kick started" to help with her dieting. 	So I 	told her
about this way of losing fat simply by standing in cold water.
[NO'l'E: 	Story/Mechanism: Story about friend, mechanism is standing
in cold water]
She thought I 	was nutzo of course ;-)
But she followed my instructions and saw results fairly quickly.
Now, 	you maybe wondering:
"Can you REALLY lose weight standing in cold water??? That sounds
crazy!"
I 	know it sounds crazy~
But, 	that doesn't mean it's not true 	:-)
The science behind it is 100% sound.
It works like this:

sufficient. ]
The result?
It FORCES your body temperature to rise.
And that burns fat!
Sometimes even a LOT of fat.
This has been documented by science.

World Class Email Tip
Straight FroiD The World's
First ''X-Rated'' Movie!
Back in community college I took an especially grueling hard course. What we'd
do is watch a movie in class (like, for example, Forest Gump or Apocalypse Now), then
-13-

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
read the book and talk about it in the next class.
Oh the agony!
Don't you feel sorry for me? I mean, that is HARD!
I don't remember learning anything in that class, of course (although I was sure
entertained!). But, one movie we watched has been invaluable in my emails today. In fact,
it helped me do something few others are able to do without looking like copycats
(especially people copying when I do it). And that is ... creating my own "language."
Words like "goo-roo" or "gluteus assimus" or "the green stuff" ... words that form my own
language (Benamese?). A language I pepper amongst my emails. And I got the idea when
thinking back about watching the classic (first ever "X-Rated") movie '~ Clockwork
Orange" in that class. The author purposely created his own unique sort of mish-mash
English-Russian language and it makes the book a lot more cool than if he hadn't.
For example, here is the opening of the book (and the movie):
"There was me, 	that is Alex, 	and my three droogs, 	that is Pete,
Georgie and Dim and we sat in the Korova milkbar trying to make
up our rassoodocks what to do with the evening. 	The Korova Milk
Bar sold milkplus, 	milk plus vellocet or synthemesc or drencrom
which is what we were drinking. 	This would sharpen you up and
make you ready for a bit of the old ultra-violence. 	Our pockets
were full of money so there was no need on that score, 	but, 	as
they say, money isn't everything."
"Droog" (which I shamelessly use in my emails sometimes). "rassoodocks" and all
the other words you see peppered throughout the rest of the book.
I DIG that kind of writing.
(And highly recommend reading the book, which is even better than the movie.)
You can easily follow the ideas without even knowing what the words mean -- as
your brain sort of fills in the meanings as you read it. And my friend, you can do this in
emails all day long. Start creating your own "lexicon" of words that are uniquely YOU.
Start with one word and use it. Then introduce another one. Then another. .. until one
day your subscribers will know you and your unique language and look forward to it.

(And your competitors will likely even shamelessly copy it, which is the mark you're doing

How To Respond To Long,
Ram.bling Complaint Mail
As many of my subscribers know, I like a good, steamy pile of hate mail on my
desk. It makes for great "fodder" for other emails, and lets you publicly mock the very
people you don't want on your list (so you scare them away, while attracting the people
you DO want on your list). But, sometimes if you do email the way I suggest you will get
not just hate mail, but dumb mail. People who are emotionally responding to you with no
point. Or maybe they are "Google bombing" you with URL's they really think you're
going to take the time to read through or whatever. One lady sent me a long, rambling
email about a single WORD I used in an email... that just rambled on and on and on.
Until recendy, my suggestion was to NEVER answer these em.alls.
Doing so is guaranteed to become a giant time and energy suck for you -- as there
are some people who have nothing better to do than get into long pissing matches via
emails or on forums. But for the rest of us, time is money. And so responding (even
reading) their nonsense is costing us some of the green stuff we'll never get back. I mean,
let's face it: it's no different than wrestling with a pig. You both get dirty and the pig
LIKES it.
However, lately, I've changed my mind on this. I have been experimenting with a
way to answer these types that:
(1) pisses them off beyond belief and ...
(2) effectively ends the conversation with you the "winner" (even if they
email you back).
Hey, I will admit there is a lot of ego involved with this. But really, if you do this
think of all the other people you're helping by planting a seed in their mind that may
-15-

THE 	EMAIL 	PLAYERS 	NEWSLETTER
prompt them to not waste anyone else's time with their stoopid driveling emails with no
point. Time really is money, after all (it ain't just a cliche'). So, instead of getting sucked
into long debates or pissing matches with people (tempting as it may be) when they send
you anything longer than a paragraph, just send this exactly "as is":

'

(Go ahead and type that link into your browser, and you'll see exactly why this not
only mocks them, but silences them, too). Funny stuff, eh? Maybe this isn't an "email" tip.
But if it saves you money and frees your mind from thinking about how to answer
someone back who is purposely trying to steal your income (by stealing your time with
their idiocy) then I think you will agree this is a very important tip for keeping and
making money. Plus, it's intensely satisfying knowing you just shut them up and there is
nothing they can say back to you that won't make them look butt-hurt or foolish.
(#winning -- ha).
Word up,
Ben Settle
